'''Slique Brighteyes''' was a [[Gulmarid]] native of [[Gulma]] who survived the atrocities committed by the [[Imperial extermination ship]] ''[[Azgoghk]]''.

==Biography==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Slique Brighteyes and other Gulmarids lived peacefully on Gulma until the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was invaded and subjugated by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] several months prior to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. The Empire harvested all available natural resources on Gulma, transformed the planet into a barren wasteland and proceeded to relocate the planet's Gulmarid population to the Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starship]] known as the ''Azgoghk''. While the Gulmarids were under false belief that the ship was assigned to transport them offworld, Brighteyes and his companions soon realized the truth behind the Empire's motives for transferring their species to the ''Azgoghk''. The ''Azgoghk'' was in fact one of the three Imperial extermination ships commissioned by the genocidal [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Danetta Pitta]] to eradicate [[Non-huMan]] species through the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and establish all-[[Human/Legends|Human]] governance over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in accordance with [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]], and thus the Empire strove for the deliberate and systematic extermination of the Gulmarids.<ref name="aod"/>

Aboard the ''Azgoghk'' the Gulmarids, like many other captive species, were frequently abused and tormented by the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Those Gulmarids who were considered weak, sick and feeble were killed by disintegrating them to base elements in the ship's ionization chambers. Brighteyes was separated from his unlucky family members, and they perished in the ionization chambers. Brighteyes and the survivors were used as forced labor assigned to dispose the deceased Gulmarids by incinerating their corpses in the ''Azgoghk''{{'s}} fusion reactors. While most of the surviving Gulmarid [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] were kept under abysmal living conditions by the [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mir Tork]], the rest were killed in the horrific experiments conducted by the science officer [[Leonis Murthé]]. However, Brighteyes and many others eventually escaped the ''Azgoghk'' and settled on the planet [[Basteel/Legends|Basteel]]. He became the spokesman of the Gulmarid refugees, planning to end the ''Azgoghk''{{'s}} reign of terror once and for all.<ref name="aod"/>

In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="BFOmnibus"/> Brighteyes collected one hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], the entire wealth of the last surviving Gulmarids, and put out a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] notice on Tork and Murthé. However, all [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] recruited by the Gulmarids were unsuccessful in boarding the massive, fortress-like extermination ship. Seeing the notorious bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] as his only hope, Brighteyes hired him to eliminate the fanatical Admiral Tork and his sadistic second-in-command Murthé. Fett scoffed his contractor for presenting such a negligible offer, considering it as a sordid insult to his pride. Brighteyes told Fett how his name used to inspire fear and reverence until Fett was defeated by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] during the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|Battle of Carkoon]]. He succeeded in appealing Fett's code of honor and justice, and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] bounty hunter was determined to redeem his failing career by accepting the Gulmarid's offer. Fett tracked down the ''Azgoghk'' in the Outer Rim Territories and took down the ship on [[Malicar 3]]. In the following skirmish, Fett liberated Non-human prisoners of the ''Azgoghk'', caused a revolt against the Imperial troops and executed his targets. After the ''Azgoghk'' was incapacitated, Fett returned to Brighteyes on Basteel with the severed heads of Tork and Murthé. Fett fulfilled his mission and was awarded with his payment. Slique Brighteyes, having survived longer than his Imperial tormentors, was finally able to rest in peace.<ref name="aod"/>
